title: JudgeResult
description: Output of stratum_judge — strict superset of CrossModelReviewResult. Adds judge-specific top-level fields (met, stakes, predicates, tier_disagreements, budget_consumed, judge_kernel_meta); inherits findings/clean/consensus/claude_only/codex_only/meta/summary unchanged. Invariant: clean == met. Findings are emitted only for not_met or ambiguous predicates; met predicates contribute zero findings.

met.description: Top-level goal verdict. Defined as all(p.verdict == 'met' for p in predicates) after per-predicate normalization. Identical to inherited `clean` by invariant.

stakes.description: Depth class set by caller. cheap (T1 only), default (T1+T2). paranoid (T1+T2, then a T3 adversary over every interpretive 'met'). STRAT-JUDGE-T3-READJAIL: the paranoid T3 is now a true cross-model jailed Codex adversary (macOS sandbox-exec read-jail confining reads to the staged turn tree) when available, else an honest probe-time degrade to the v1 in-process Claude cold-read. Per-predicate provenance is in predicates[].t3.

predicates.description: Per-predicate verdicts. MAY be empty for zero-turn results (e.g. budget exhausted before any judge call). Non-empty results are validated at the kernel boundary (EmptyPredicateListError) to prevent vacuous-pass gate bypass.

type.description: Predicate type sets the tier ceiling. deterministic = T1 only. verified = T2. judged = T2, plus T3 cold-read adversary when stakes=paranoid (else T2-terminal; judge_kernel_meta.degraded_judged flags judged predicates that got no adversary).

verdict.description: Post-normalization verdict. A raw 'met' with confidence < applied_gate is downgraded to 'ambiguous' before this is written.

applied_gate.description: Per-predicate confidence threshold. Defaults to 7 (mirrors review-finding convention).

source.description: Canonical citation format <bucket>/<path>:<line>. bucket must be 'artifacts' or 'modified'. Validated at the kernel boundary; path must resolve under the current turn's staging tree.

confidence.description: Null for T1 'n/a' verdicts (no LLM tier ran).

t3.description: STRAT-JUDGE-T3-READJAIL. Populated ONLY when a T3 adversary was reached for this predicate (stakes='paranoid' AND T2='met'); null/absent means no adversary ran for this predicate (the absence is itself the honest signal). Descriptive of what actually ran, never intent. Authoritative T3 model identity is t3.model_id — top-level meta.model_id is the T1/T2 worker-lane identity only.

mode.description: codex_jailed: true cross-model Codex adversary, OS read-jailed (macOS sandbox-exec) to the staged turn tree. claude_cold_fallback: probe-time degrade to in-process Claude cold-read (no OS jail; reasoning-isolation+ordering only). codex_jailed_error: jailed adversary failed post-launch — predicate resolved ambiguous, error surfaced, NEVER silently relabeled fallback.

model_id.description: The actual model that ran T3 (authoritative for the adversary lane).

residual.description: The accepted residual for this mode, verbatim from the design Problem statement. Never rounded up to 'confined'.

tier_disagreements.description: Cross-tier disagreements within a single judge call. Populated when stakes=paranoid and T3 contradicts a T2 'met' (resolved to 'ambiguous'; T4 quorum deferred). Empty for cheap/default.

dollars.description: Recorded but NOT enforced in v1 (no token-cost accounting yet).

decomposer_mode.description: v1 ships 'user' only; auto/hybrid/ask are v2.

smartmemory_priors_consulted.description: Always 0 in v1 (SmartMemory wiring is v2).

degraded_judged.description: True when a 'judged' predicate did not receive adversarial (T3) verification — i.e. stakes was cheap/default so T2 was terminal. False once T3 ran (stakes=paranoid).
